rulings.Case Title: WING WO MA VS. JUN TAN et al

rulings.Case Title: HERBERT LEE VS. WEBCOR BUILDERS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Enforce Written Settlement Agreement Nder Ccp 664.6; Request For Costs And Attorneys Fees Pursuant To Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 19, 2011, Line 7, DEFENDANT WEBCOR BUILDERS, INC., JESUS PULIDO Notice Of Motion To Enforce Written Settlement Agreement Nder Ccp 664.6; Request For Costs And Attorneys Fees Pursuant To Settlement Agreement - The Court grants this motion. The settlement agreement that the parties executed at the mediation is binding. See Gavina v. Smith, (1944) 25 Cal.2d 501, 504. The Court denies defendant's request for fees. Par. 6 of the settlement agreement provides that each party will bear its own litigation costs and attorney fees. Par. 10 does not provide for indemnification in connection with this motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ROGELIO HECHAVARRIA VS. ELIAS GEORGOPOULOS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 19, 2011, Line 7, PLAINTIFF ROGELIO HECHAVARRIA's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery And For Sanctions. Denied as to request for production nos. 31-34. The recorded interviews taken by the investigator are privileged. Request for privilege log is denied as moot, since Defendant has now provided a privilege log. Granted, with 10 days to respond, as to requests for admission nos. 10 - 13. Defendant shall admit, deny or specify why he lacks specific knowledge as to respond to each request. Denied as to requests for admission nos. 14-35. Plaintiff failed to set forth the substance of any discovery in the Separate Statement. In any event, Defendant has since submitted supplemental responses with the requested language sought by the motion, so the motion is moot. Denied as to "supplemental interrogatory to form interrogatory no. 12.3," which is mentioned in the notice of motion, but not discussed in the Separate Statement, or in substance in the memorandum of points and authorities. Requests for sanctions are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM))

rulings.Case Title: SUSAN Y F LEE VS. WAH-BUN LEE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 19, 2011, Line 19, DEFENDANTs TONY LEE, LAI-SAU LEE's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer is overruled in its entirety. Plaintiff's conspiracy allegations are sufficient. Reading the First Amended Complaint as a whole, plaintiff alleges that both Tony Lee and Lai-Sau Lee were part of the conspiracy and they can be liable for the acts that Wah-Bun Lee allegedly took to further the conspiracy. The Court rejects the statute of limitations argument because even if some of the acts are time barred, defendants failed to show that the causes of action are barred in their entirety. Plaintiff sufficiently pleads a landlord tenant relationship with Ms. Lai-Sau Lee. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM))

rulings.Case Title: SUSAN Y F LEE VS. WAH-BUN LEE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 19, 2011, Line 19, DEFENDANT WAH-BUN LEE's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer is overruled in its entirety. The 1st through 6th causes of action are not uncertain. Plaintiff sufficiently pleads the existence of a conspiracy and Mr. Wah-Bun Lee is liable for the acts that he took to further the conspiracy. There is no basis to strike the conspiracy allegations and the motion to strike is denied. The demurrer to the 7th cause of action for negligence is also overruled; plaintiff's allegations that Mr. Wah-Bun Lee is a landlord are sufficient, and it states a cause of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM))
